GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Width of item. TIP: Use Width to validate conditions for the Number variables. EXAMPLE: Setting the appropriate number of door pulls by using a formula. The NDWP variable would have the following formula: Cond(Width<=30,1,2) Explanation: Condition, if the width is less than or equal to 30, add 1 pull otherwise 2 pulls.

GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Height of an item. TIP: Use Height to validate conditions for the Number variables. EXAMPLE: Setting the appropriate number of shelves in a cabinet by using a formula. The NS1 variable would have the following formula: Round(Height/18,1) Explanation: The cabinet height is divided by 18, the end result will determine the number of shelves to be added. If applied on an item that is 42" high, the calculation would be 42 / 18 = 2.3, the 2.3 is rounded down to 2.

GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Depth of an item. TIP: Use the Depth to validate conditions for the Drawer variables. EXAMPLE: Setting the appropriate depth of a drawer in a cabinet by using a formula. The DWD1 variable would have the following formula: Depth-4 Explanation: The depth of the drawer will be the depth of the cabinet less 4". If applied to an item that is 24" in depth, the calculation would be 24 - 4 = 20.

GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Maximum width. Used to set a limit of the maximum dimension applicable to the width of an item. TIP: Use a formula in your Catalog.SYM file to set the maximum dimension for all cabinet widths. EXAMPLE: Setting the maximum dimension applicable to all items throughout the catalog. In your Catalog.SYM file set the MAXW variable with the following formula: Width Explanation: The maximum width applicable to an item will be the initial width it was created with. This will block the possibility of increasing the width of items in the Design program.

GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Maximum depth. Used to set a limit of the maximum dimension applicable to the depth of an item. TIP: Use a formula in your Catalog.SYM file to set the maximum dimension for all cabinet depths. EXAMPLE: Setting the maximum dimension applicable to all items throughout the catalog. In your Catalog.SYM file set the MAXD variable with the following formula: Depth Explanation: The maximum depth applicable to an item will be the initial depth it was created with. This will block the possibility of increasing the width of the items in the Design program.

GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Maximum height. Used to set a limit of the maximum dimension applicable to the height of an item. TIP: Use a formula in your Catalog.SYM file to set the maximum dimension for all cabinet heights. EXAMPLE: Setting the maximum dimension applicable to all items throughout the catalog. In your Catalog.SYM file set the MAXH variable with the following formula: Height Explanation: The maximum height applicable to an item will be the initial height it was created with. This will block the possibility of increasing the height of the items in the Design program.

GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Minimum width. Used to set a limit on the minimum dimension applicable to the width of an item. TIP: Use a formula in your Catalog.SYM file to set the minimum dimension for all cabinet widths. EXAMPLE: Setting the minimum dimension applicable to 2 15/16" less than the initial width the items that were created with through out the catalog. In your Catalog.SYM file set the MINW variable with the following formula: width-2.9375 Explanation: The minimum width applicable to an item will be the initial width it was created with, less 2 15/16". This will block the possibility of decreasing the width of items beyond 2 15/16" in the Design program. When applied to an item that is 18” wide, the width cannot be decreased below 15 1/16”.

GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Minimum depth. Used to set a limit of the minimum dimension applicable to the depth of an item. TIP: Use a formula in your Catalog.SYM file to set the minimum dimensions for all cabinet depths. EXAMPLE: Setting the minimum dimension applicable to 3" less than the initial depth the items that were created through out the catalog. In your Catalog.SYM file set the MIND variable with the following formula: Depth-3 Explanation: The minimum depth applicable to an item will be the initial depth it was created with, less 3". This will block the possibility of decreasing the depth of the items beyond 3" in the Design program. When applied to an item that is 24" deep, the depth cannot be decreased below 21".

GroupDescription: Overall and interior dimensions. Values required in this group represent dimensions, except when specified.
Description: Minimum height. Used to set a limit of the minimum dimension applicable to the height of an item. TIP: Use a formula in your Catalog.SYM file to set the minimum dimensions for all cabinet heights. EXAMPLE: Setting the minimum dimension applicable to 3" less than the initial height the items that were created through out the catalog. In your Catalog.SYM file set the MINH variable with the following formula: Height-3 Explanation: The minimum height applicable to an item will be the initial height it was created with, less 3". This will block the possibility of decreasing the height of the items beyond 3" in the Design program. When applied to an item that is 15" high, the height cannot be decreased below 15".

Example: On an base island cabinet, you may want to attach a roll-out that is will slide through the back of the cabinet, the value of the ATR variable would be set as 1.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Interior width of an item.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Interior height of an item.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Interior depth of an item.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: End cabinet radius. Diagonal dimension from miter cut to back corner (square corner projection). TIP: Maximum radius is the diagonal length between Width and Depth of cabinet.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Radius for end curved cabinets - convex (i.e. curved out). TIP: The larger the number, the greater the curve.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Radius for end curved cabinets - concave (i.e. curved in). TIP: The larger the number, the greater the curve.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Depth of shelves. If used in a configurable item, then the shelf depth is of the first section, if on the horizontal plane the sections start from left to right and if on a vertical plane the sections start from bottom towards the top. TIP: This may be used in conjunction with the ConfHeight1 variable.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Depth of shelves for second cabinet section, if on the horizontal plane the sections start from left to right and if on the vertical plane the sections start from bottom towards the top. TIP: May work in conjunction with the ConfHeight2 variable.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Depth of shelves for third cabinet section, if on the horizontal plane the sections start from left to right and if on the vertical plane the sections start from bottom towards the top. TIP: May work in conjunction with the ConfHeight3 variable.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Depth of shelves for fourth cabinet section, if on the horizontal plane the sections start from left to right and if on the vertical plane the sections start from bottom towards the top. TIP: May work in conjunction with the ConfHeight4 variable.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Depth of shelves for fifth cabinet section, if on the hoizontal plane the sections start from left to right and if on the vertical plane the sections start from bottom towards the top. TIP: May work in conjunction with the ConfHeight5 variable.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Front cut corner of a 45?angle (i.e. clipped corner). TIP: The value entered is the width of the clipped corner.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Space of squares for lattice (opening size). TIP: This works in conjunction with the WRSW variable.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Lattice separator width (thickness of material used to create separators). TIP: This works in conjunction with the WRSS variable.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: To explode the components of a cabinet. Specify the space required between the components. If value is set as 0, standard display of item, if value is set as 1, exploded view of item.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Used to switch between standard and peninsula cabinets. If the value is set as 0, the unit is set as standard, if the value is set as 1 the unit is set as peninsula.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Groove depth for receiving the back panel on a side panel. TIP: For imperial units, the maximum groove depth is the panel side thickness minus 1/8". Formula: ST-1/8 For metric units, the maximum groove depth is the panel side thickness minus 3mm. Formula: ST-3

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Angle on an angle cabinet.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Height of the material used as an internal divider, for dividers that are not full-height.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Depth of the material used as an internal divider, for dividers that are not full-depth.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Calculates the Finished side(s) of cabinet(s) within a design globally. The values represent the following: -5, the system verifies if the right side needs to be set as finished and sets the left side as finished, -4, the system verifies if the right side needs to be set as finished and sets the left side as unfinished, -3, the system verifies if the left side needs to be set as finished and sets the right side as finished, -2, the system verifies if the left side needs to be set as finished and sets the right side as unfinished, -1, the system verifies if both the left and right sides need to be finished, 0, the left and right sides are set as unfinished, 1, the left side is set as finished and the right side as unfinished, 2, the right side is set as finished and the left side as unfinished, 3, the left and right sides are set as finished.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Depth of pull-out table attached to a cabinet.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Depth of an adjustable floor. Example: On a sink front.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Second depth of an adjustable floor. Example: On a sink front.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Finished edge on finished side(s) of a unit. If the value is set at 0, there will not be a finished edge on a finished side, if the value is set at 1, a finished edge will be set on the finished side. This variable works in conjunction with the SFH variable.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Displays a cut corner in the front right corner of the item. If the value entered is 1 a clipped right corner is displayed, if the value entered is 0 a standard corner is displayed.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Displays a cut corner in the front left corner of the item. If value entered is 1 a clipped left corner is displayed, if the value entered is 0 a standard corner is displayed.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Used to calculate the Finished Sides. It is also used to overwrite the user selection and the SFH variable. The values represent the following: 1, Charges according to the user selection. 2, Does not charge finished sides regardless of the user selection. 3, Charges one side regardless of the user selection. 4, One charge is applied once there is at least one Finish Side set. 5, Two charges are applied once there is at least one Finish Side set. 6, Two charges are applied regardless of the user selection.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Leg position on the Z plane. (In, Out)

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Leg style.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Leg position on the X plane. (Left, Right)

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Display leg.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Leg height.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Leg quantity.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: * Display interior finish.

GroupDescription: Shelf depths, radius, cut corners and special configurations.
Description: Side finish neighbor type. To specify the side finish depending on the type of neighbor. Variable bitfield (Appliance,Wall,Obstacle).
Clearance behind the cabinet back panel, when there are support brackets. Explanation: Horizontal clearance between shelves and box necessary for adjustable peg system.
Clearance under counter tops. Explanation: Clearance between the top of the doors and the bottom of the counter top in frameless cabinets.
Clearance between two horizontal sections. Explanation: Clearance for doors between 2 horizontal sections in frameless cabinets.
Back door/drawer side clearance.
Door/drawer side horizontal clearance.
Vertical clearance between door and drawer.
Horizontal clearance between two doors in frameless cabinets.
Vertical clearance between two doors in frameless cabinets.
Vertical bottom clearance between the drawer case and the drawer front.
Horizontal difference between the total width of the drawer case and the interior width of the cabinet (horizontal side clearance for drawers).
Horizontal clearance between 2 drawers in frameless cabinets.
Clearance above the top of cabinet and under the floor, this recesses the top/bottom in relation to the sides.
Horizontal clearance between shelves and box necessary for adjustable peg system.
Horizontal clearance between face frame and box for finished ends Example: Face frame side overhang.
Tolerance necessary when back is imbedded into the sides, tops and floors.
Vertical clearance between the top of the door and the box in frameless cabinets.
Horizontal clearance between the bottom of the door and the box in frameless cabinets.
Horizontal clearance between two butt doors in frame cabinets.
Top clearance for appliances.
Bottom clearance for appliances.
Vertical clearance between two J moldings.
Vertical clearance between 6th and 7th drawer.
Vertical clearance between 5th and 6th drawer.
Vertical clearance between 4th and 5th drawer.
Vertical clearance between 3rd and 4th drawer.
Vertical clearance between 2nd and 3rd drawer.
Clearance under the floor of the cabinet, this recesses the bottom in relation to the sides.
Clearance above the top of the cabinet, this recesses the top in relation to the sides.
Tolerance required for cabinets, when the second side is not of the same depth as the first side.
Horizontal clearance between face frame and box for finished ends Example: Face frame side overhang.
Vertical clearance between two J moldings.

GroupDescription: Vertical and horizontal delimiters for cabinet sections TIP: A fixed divider or a fixed shelf always separates a section. Sections represent either a horizontal (from left to right) or vertical (from bottom to top) assembly of cabinets. Example: Door sytle, drawer style, wine rack.
Description: Height of the lowest vertical section (door or opening) of an item. TIP: To know the measure of the upper wall door when you change the ConfHeights, use the following formula: Total cabinet height - ConfHeights = Top wall cabinet height. Example: If the total cabinet height = 84", the ConfHeight1 = 34.5" and ConfHeight2 = 31.5", then the calculation is as the follows: 84" - 34.5" - 31.5" = 18", which is the height of the top section.

GroupDescription: Vertical and horizontal delimiters for cabinet sections TIP: A fixed divider or a fixed shelf always separates a section. Sections represent either a horizontal (from left to right) or vertical (from bottom to top) assembly of cabinets. Example: Door sytle, drawer style, wine rack.
Description: Height of the second vertical section (door or opening) of an item, starting from the bottom, upwards. The measurement of the CH2 is positioned starting from half the thickness of the first fixed shelf. TIP: To know the measure of the upper wall door when you change the ConfHeights, use the following formula: Total cabinet height - ConfHeights = Top wall cabinet height Example: If the total cabinet height = 84", the ConfHeight1 = 34.5" and ConfHeight2 = 31.5", then the calculation is as the follows: 84" - 34.5" - 31.5" = 18", which is the height of the top section.

Description: Width of the first horizontal section, starting from the left. TIP: You can use formulas to set the configuration widths. If you have an item that has three section and wish to have the first and second section equal, and the middle section with a fixed width. The formula would be as follows: Total cabinet width - fixed width, then divide the remainder by 2; (W-12)/2 Example: Total cabinet Width = 60", the fixed width of the middle section = 12, the first and last section have equal values. The calculation is as follows: (60" - 12 ") / 2 = 24", which is the width of the first and third section.

Description: To add components to the first section (starting from the left or the bottom) of a configurable unit. TIP: Enter one or more of the following values to make a combination: SHLF = shelves, DIV = divider, VAL = valance and/or a numerical value for a type of configuration (doors, drawers, wine racks...). Use the plus sign (+) to add more than one value. (To test, use composition 70002.) EXAMPLE:: If SGC1 = 1 + SHLF, you will obtain full height doors and shelves.

Description: Height of the sixth vertical section (door or opening), starting from the bottom. The base of CH6 corresponds to half the thickness of the fifth fixed shelf. TIP: To know the measure of the upper wall door when you change the ConfHeights, use the following formula: Total cabinet height - ConfHeights = Top wall cabinet height Example: If the total cabinet height = 84", the ConfHeight1 = 34.5" and ConfHeight2 = 31.5", then the calculation is as follows: 84" - 34.5" - 31.5" = 18", which is the height of the top section.

Description: System variable that allows the alignment of a standalone item to its logical owner. Example: When an add-on sink gets assigned to a base cabinet, the BaseAlign variable takes into consideration the countertop thickness when calculating the exact vertical position of the sink. This is possible since the add-on has access to its owner’s variables. If, on the other hand, a standalone sink must be placed on top of a cabinet, the countertop thickness isn’t available to estimate the up/down value required to align the sink with the cabinet. In this context, specifying PRoprioAlign instead of BaseAlign instructs the program to determine the logical owner of the standalone item and to use the relevant owner’s information to align the de facto add-on item properly.

When a vertical section has 2 doors stacked one on top of the other, this is the height of the bottom door. TIP: The height of the top door is calculated by subtracting DOH1 from CH1.
When a vertical section is composed of 3 doors, height of the middle door. TIP: The height of the top door is calculated by subtracting DOH1 and DOH2 from CH1.
Height of top and bottom door frame.
Width of left and right door frame.
Door width 1.
Door width 2.
Maximum width of filler, which when exceeded makes a plain filler become a filler with a door style.

Custom X origin for door and drawer pulls. Tip: This variable works in conjunction with the door/drawer pull positions (Style category: PPB, PPW, PPT1, PPDW, PPTO). You need to set these variables at the CUSTOM locator number. The program calculates the new X origin according to the X origin of the STANDARD pull positions.

Custom Y origin for door and drawer pulls. Tip: This variable works in conjunction with the door/drawer pull positions (Style category: PPB, PPW, PPT1, PPDW, PPTO). You need to set these variables at the CUSTOM locator number. The program calculates the new Y origin according to the Y origin of the STANDARD pull positions.

Custom angle for door and drawer pulls. Tip: This variable works in conjunction with the door/drawer pull positions (Style category: PPB, PPW, PPT1, PPDW, PPTO). You need to set these variables at the CUSTOM locator number.
Width of chamfer on doors and drawer fronts.
Width of panel chamfer on doors and drawer fronts.

Specifies the presence of two vertical panels on the same base door. Tip: If the value is set as 1, then 2 vertical panels are set on a base door, If the value is set as 0, then 1 vertical panel is set on a base door.

Specifies the presence of two vertical panels on the same wall door. Tip: If the value is set as 1, then 2 vertical panels are set on a wall door, if the value is set as 0, then 1 vertical panel is set on a wall door.

Specifies the presence of two vertical panels on the same tall door. Tip: If the value is set as 1, then 2 vertical panels are set on a tall door, if the value is set as 0, then 1 vertical panel is set on a tall door.
Specifies the presence of a clock on a door. Tip: If the value is set as 1, add a clock on the cabinet door, if the value is set as 0, do not add clock on the cabinet door.

Specifies the presence of two horizontal panels on the same door if the Width of the door is greater or equal to DHDP (W >= DHDP). Example: If a door is wider than the dimension value set in the DHDP, a Double Horizontal Door Panel will be displayed.
Specifies the number of vertical panels on a door.
Door Hinge clearance.
Door Hinge bottom location in height.
Door Hinge top location in height.

GroupDescription: These variables are system variables that can only be seen in the Locator Editor screen: you add these by clicking on Newvar. Their role is to optimize compositions, for instance by increasing the graphic display speed and removing access to unnecessary operations. Note: These variables are affected in run-time only.
Description: This variable indicates to the program to load the locator number which is indicated on LocSelect (instead of reading all the locators listed). Example: Instead of evaluating ALL the locator base pull positions, you can indicate the locator number you wish to use, for instance the standard position, without the program having to scan all the related locators.

GroupDescription: These variables are system variables that can only be seen in the Locator Editor screen: you add these by clicking on Newvar. Their role is to optimize compositions, for instance by increasing the graphic display speed and removing access to unnecessary operations. Note: These variables are affected in run-time only.
Description: This variable indicates to the program the last variable to read from a longer list. It is placed on the last locator you want the program to read. Usually, it is at the end of a series of locators that have something in common. This is a logical variable, which means that if the result of LocBreak is 0, it is false and the program continues reading the locators listed. However, if the result of LocBreak is 1, then it is true and the program stops reading from the list. Example: If you have locators related to 1 or 2 doors, some to 3 doors and others to 4 doors, you can "break" at the end of the 1-2 doors and also at the end of the 3 doors. Once the LocBreak value is true (equals 1), the program stops reading from the list. In other words, this means the whole list does not need to be evaluated.

GroupDescription: Style for doors, drawers, pulls..., as well as pull positions.
Description: To specify the presence or absence of toe kicks on standard cabinets or peninsulas. The bval expression must be used in the following manner: bval(FB) where you replace the letters - F (for Front) and B (for Back) - by 0 (absence) OR 1 (presence). Example: If STK = Bval(11), then the program would draw a front and a back toe kick on the cabinet. On custom cabinets, 2 other sides may be specified, the expression becomes: bval(FBLR). Replace the letters - F (for Front), B (for Back), L (for Left) and R (for Right) - by 0 (absence) OR 1 (presence). Example: If STK = Bval(1001), then the program would draw a toe kick on the front and right side of the cabinet.

Tip: 1=Side/Center/Vert, 2=Side/Center/Hor, 3=Side/Center/Diag.R, 4=Side/Center/Diag.L, 5=Side/Bottom/Vert, 6=Side/Bottom/Hor, 7=Side/Bottom/Diag.R, 8=Side/Bottom/Diag.L, 9=Side/Top/Vert, 10=Side/Top/Hor, 11=Side/Top/Diag.R, 12=Side/Top/Diag.L, 13=Center/Center/Vert, 14=Center/Center/Hor, 15=Center/Center/Diag.R, 16=Center/Center/Diag.L, 17=Center/Top/Hor, 18=Center/Bottom/Hor, 19=No Pull.

GroupDescription: Style for doors, drawers, pulls..., as well as pull positions.
Description: Cabinet box assembly model. Tip: Models for face frame cabinets: 1 = 4 triangular supports and 1 back rail, 2 = top and toe box, 3 = top, 4 = top and adjustable floor, 5 = 2 triangular supports and back rail, 6 = side rails. Models for frameless: 1 = 2 rails, 2 = top and toe box, 3 = top.

GroupDescription: Style for doors, drawers, pulls..., as well as pull positions.
Description: Vertical quantity of drawers. Tip: The number of drawers must be between 0 and 7.

GroupDescription: Style for doors, drawers, pulls..., as well as pull positions.
Description: Base door model. Tip: Enter a door style number and, if desired, add “+glass” to display glass doors on the model selected. Example: If SDB = 5 + glass, you would see glass doors on the base door model number 5. If you enter 0 + glass, you obtain frameless doors with glass panels.

GroupDescription: Style for doors, drawers, pulls..., as well as pull positions.
Description: Tall door model. Tip: Enter a door style number and, if desired, add “+glass” to display glass doors on the model selected. Example: If SDT1 = 5 + glass, you would see glass doors on the tall door model number 5. If you enter 0+glass, you obtain frameless doors with glass panels.

GroupDescription: Style for doors, drawers, pulls..., as well as pull positions.
Description: Wall door model. Tip: Enter a door style number and, if desired, add “+glass” to display glass doors on the model selected. Example: If SDW = 5 + glass, you would see glass doors on the wall door model number 5. If you enter 0 + glass, you obtain frameless doors with glass panels.

GroupDescription: Style for doors, drawers, pulls..., as well as pull positions.
Description: Drawer front model. Tip: Enter a door style number and, if desired, add “+glass” to display glass panels on the model selected. Example: If SDRW = 5 + glass, you would see glass panels on the drawer model number 5.

Unit of pricing, this variable is of great importance to the Design program. When used correctly, it allows to accurately predict how many pieces or lengths of top moldings, bottom moldings, toe kicks and counter tops to be used in the design. TIP: The UnitOfPricing variable works in conjunction with the RoundUPMeasure variable. You should assign the length of one piece of material in the UnitOfPricing variable. Example: If you offer this item in lengths of 96” only, then assign 96.00. You should remember that the Price variables must match the linear length specified in the UnitOfPricing variable. In the case of the 96” sells for $100.00, then assign this value to the Price variable.

Round up measure, this field is of great importance to the Design program. When used correctly, it allows to accurately predicting how each section of moldings, toe kick and counter top will be rounded off to. TIP: For instance, if you have a range separating cabinets, the program interprets this as two sections for the same wall. When the report calculations begin, the Design program establishes how many sections of the cabinets are present. In this situation, the RoundUPMeasure variable value would guide the program to round off each sectional length. If to the left of the range you had a 1 ? filler and a 30" wide cabinet the record selected had a value of 1.00 on RoundUPMeasure, then the program would consider this section as being 32" instead of 31 ?. The program verifies the value assigned on RoundUPMeasure to round each section on the work area. The rounded section lengths are added up and compared to the linear length (UnitOFPricing). Last, the program calculates the number of linear lengths required. Example: Section 1 measures 32" and section 2, 87". These sections are rounded using the RoundUPMeasure variable (12). Section 1 becomes 36" and section 2 becomes 96". The rounded values are added up and total 132". Last, the program verifies the linear length on UnitOFPricing (96") and charges two lengths at $100.00 a piece.

GroupDescription: Variables for Enhancer Objects. (Countertops and trims)
Description: Minimum distance for contiguity criteria. Tip: If value is set as 0 the countertop will follow the shape of the items. If a dimension is set as a value, then this range will cover (fill in) the normally empty space. Example: Two items are aligned to the back of a wall, first item is placed, the second item is placed 6 inches to the right of the first item. Leaving a gap of 6" in width. If the value of EOMDC is set as 6, when adding a countertop to these items you will get one slap of a full length without any gaps. Space between two cabinets supported to continue the linear item.

GroupDescription: Variables for Enhancer Objects. (Countertops and trims)
Description: Max. distance allowed from a wall. Fills in the space between the back of the cabinet and the wall. Space between two cabinets supported to continue the linear item. Tip: If value is set as 0 the countertop will follow the shape of the items. If a dimension is set as a value, then this range will cover (fill in) the normally extruded space. Example: Three items are aligned to the back of a wall first item is of 24"in depth, the second items is of 21"in depth and is moved out in order to align with the others, the third is of 24" in depth. If the value of EOMDFW is set as 3, when adding a countertop to these items you will get one slap of a full depth without any extrusions.

GroupDescription: Variables for Enhancer Objects. (Countertops and trims)
Description: Type of cut. Tip: If value is set as: 0 = Miter, 1 = Straight, 2 = Straight max depth, 3 = Double, 4 = Bullnose, 5 = Diagonal, 6 = Diagonal with cut, and 7 = none.

GroupDescription: Variables for Enhancer Objects. (Countertops and trims)
Description: For linear items only such as moldings, toe kicks, aliases/trims, etc. Allows the program to draw trims continually around units even if they have different placement heights. Tip: When value is set as 0, normal placement, when value is set as 1, place vertically and horizontally.

GroupDescription: Variables for Enhancer Objects. (Countertops and trims)
Description: Minimum vector length is set as a manufacturers’ constraint, it is the smallest feature a saw can create. Tip: If value is set as 0 the countertop will follow the shape of the items. If a dimension is set as a value, then this range will cover (fill in) the normally indented space. Example: Three items are aligned to the back of a wall first item is of 24"in depth, the second items is of 21"in depth and the third is of 24" in depth. If the value of MINVL is set as 3, when adding a countertop to these items, you will get one slap of a full depth without any indentation.

GroupDescription: Variables for Enhancer Objects. (Countertops and trims)

Value set opens and closes the doors per configuration. Tip: Value entered is applied in degrees. When the value is set as 0 the displayed doors are closed. Tip: This is an array variable, which can be used to a maximum of 10 configurations within an item. When applied to an item with one configuration but contains multiple doors the value applied in the DOP will affect all the doors of its respective section. Example: If aplied to an item that has one configuration which contains five doors,if DOP[1]=45, then the five doors will be open at a 45degree angle. If applied to an item with several configurations each section contains its respective DOP variable. Example: If applied to an item that has five configurations and each configuration contains a door, then the following will used; DOP[1], DOP[2], DOP[3], DOP[4] and DOP[5].

Used to set the top door panel style. When used on door which contain more than one panel, The following values represent the door panel styles available: 500/1500/2500 = Square, 501/1501/2501 = Arch Top, 502/1502/2502 = Arch Bottom 503/1503/2503 = Arch Top & Bottom, 504/1504/2504 = Cathedral Top, 505/1505/2505 = Cathedral Bottom, 506/1506/2506 = Cathedral, 507/1507/2507 = Cathedral #2 Top 508/1508/2508 = Cathedral #2 Bottom Top & Bottom 509/1509/2509 = Cathedral #2 Arch #2 Top 510/1510/2510 = Arch #2 Bottom Top & Bottom 511/1511/2511 = Arch #2 Top & Bottom Tip: This is an array variable, it can appear in the following manner: DCPS1 [1] "1" is the array for Base Doors DCPS1 [2] "2" is the array for Wall Doors DCPS1 [3] "3" is the array for Tall Doors DCPS1 [4] "4" is the array for Drawers DCPS1 [5] "5" is the array for Raised Panels Values set between 0 – 500 are used for Flat doors, values set between 501 – 1000 are used for Arch Flat doors, values set between 1001 – 1500 are used for Square Raised doors, values set between 1501 – 2000 are used for Arch Raised doors, values set between 2001 – 2500 are used for Square Glass doors, values set between 2501 – 3000 are used for Arch Glass doors.

Used to set the bottom door panel style. Used to set the center door panel style. When used on door which contain more than one panel, The following values represent the door panel styles available: 500/1500/2500 = Square, 501/1501/2501 = Arch Top, 502/1502/2502 = Arch Bottom 503/1503/2503 = Arch Top & Bottom, 504/1504/2504 = Cathedral Top, 505/1505/2505 = Cathedral Bottom, 506/1506/2506 = Cathedral, 507/1507/2507 = Cathedral #2 Top 508/1508/2508 = Cathedral #2 Bottom Top & Bottom 509/1509/2509 = Cathedral #2 Arch #2 Top 510/1510/2510 = Arch #2 Bottom Top & Bottom 511/1511/2511 = Arch #2 Top & Bottom Tip: This is an array variable, it can appear in the following manner: DCPS3 [1] "1" is the array for Base Doors DCPS3 [2] "2" is the array for Wall Doors DCPS3 [3] "3" is the array for Tall Doors DCPS3 [4] "4" is the array for Drawers DCPS3 [5] "5" is the array for Raised Panels Values set between 0 – 500 are used for Flat doors, values set between 501 – 1000 are used for Arch Flat doors, values set between 1001 – 1500 are used for Square Raised doors, values set between 1501 – 2000 are used for Arch Raised doors, values set between 2001 – 2500 are used for Square Glass doors, values set between 2501 – 3000 are used for Arch Glass doors.

Value entered determines the location of hinges on an item. When used on an item which has multiple configurations HGC[1] determines the location of hinges found on the first configuration. When used on a peninsula item it determines the location of hinges found on the front of the item. Tip: This is an array variable, which can be used up to a maximum of 10 configurations within an item. On an item with one door, the value 0 sets the hinging to left; the value 1 sets the hinging to right. When applied to an item which has more than one door, depending on the item configuration you may have more than on HGC variable. Example: A Base Cabinet 2 door, if configured with Door Qty = 2, then you will use HGC[1] but if you use an item which is configured with two sections then you will set a value for HGC[1] and HGC[2]. It uses a binary system in order to set the location of the hinging, 0 sets the hinging to left and 1 sets the hinging to right. Thus for multiple doors on an item you may use the following formula. Example: If using one configuration HGC[1]=BVAL(00001). If using multiple configurations HGC[1]=0, HGC[2]=0, HGC[3]=0, HGC[4]=1, HGC[5]=1 This will display an item with the following hinge configuration;left, left, left, right, right. If using one configuration HGC[1]=BVAL(10101). If using multiple configurations HGC[1]=1, HGC[2]=0, HGC[3]=1, HGC[4]=0, HGC[5]=1. This will display an item with the following hinge configuration; right, left, right, left, right.

Changes the location of the origin point on the Y-axis (up/down) thus, changes the location of the floor of an item. Tip: Positive values changes the origin upwards, while negative values change the origin downward.

Changes the location of the origin point on the Z-axis (in/out) thus, changes the dimension of the depth of the floor of an item. For a corner/diagonal item it changes the first depth of the floor. Tip: Dimension change starts from the left of the item, positive values decrease the size while negative values increase.
Changes the dimension of the floor width. For a corner/diagonal item it changes the second depth if the floor. Tip: Dimension change starts from the right of the item.
Changes the second width of the floor for a corner/diagonal item.

Changes the location of the origin point on the X-axis (left/right) thus, changes the dimension of the first top width. Tip: Dimension changes start from the front, it lengthens/shortens the front right top of an item (inward, outward). A positive value reduces the dimension, where as negative values increases the dimensions. Used in conjunction with the variables TOZ and TORG.
Changes the position of the top of an item on the Y-axis (up/down). Tip: A positive value moves the top of the item upwards, where as negative values moves the top downwards.

Changes the location of the origin point on the Z-axis (in/out) thus, changes the dimension second width of the top of an item. Tip: Dimension changes start from the back, it lengthens/shortens the back top of an item (inward, outward). Used in conjunction with the variables TOX and TORG.
Changes the dimension of the top width of the item. Tip: Dimension change start from the back right of the item.
Changes the dimension for the first top depth. Tip: Dimension change starts from the front, lengthens/shortens the front left top of an item. Used in conjunction with the variable TPD2.

Changes the location of the origin point on the Y-axis (up/down) thus, changes the height of the back of an item. For corner/diagonal item it changes all the backs. Tip: Dimension changes start from the bottom of the item; positive values decrease the size while negative values increase.
Changes the thickness of the all the nailers on an item.
Changes the height of the back of an item. For a corner or diagonal item it changes the first back. Tip: Dimension change starts from the top of the item.
Changes the dimension height of the top nailer. Tip: Dimension changes start form the bottom
Changes the dimension height of the bottom nailer. Tip: Dimension changes start form the bottom

Changes the location of the origin point on the X-axis (left/right) thus, changes the dimension of the width of the back of an item. For a corner/diagonal item it changes the first back. Tip: Dimension changes start from the left of the item; positive values decrease the size while negative values increase.
Height of intermediate nailers.

Changes the placement of the intermediate nailers starting from the bottom of the item. Tip: If the value is set as 0 the intermediate nailer(s) is not displayed . This is used in conjunction with the variable NIO2.

Changes the placement of the distance between the lower and upper intermediate nailers. Tip: Placement changes start from the bottom of the previous nailer. If a value is set to 0 nailers are removed. This is used in conjunction with the variable NIO.
Changes the location of the top nailer on the X-axis (left/right). Tip: Used in conjunction with the variables NOY and NOZ. Shapes System Variable.
Changes the Width of left and/or right stiles. Tip: this is an array variable, which can be read in the follwing manner: PSTW[1], "1" is for the left style PSTW[2], "2" is for the right style.
Changes the dimension value of the height of the back toe kick. Tip: Dimension changes start from the top. Shapes System Variable.
Changes the width of the back of an item, starting from the right.
Changes the dimension value of the height of the front toe kick. Tip: Dimension changes start from the top. Shapes System Variable.
Changes the location of the top nailer on the Y-axis (up/down). Tip: Used in conjunction with the variables NOX and NOZ. Shapes System Variable.
Changes the dimension of the nailer width. Tip: Dimension changes start from the right. Shapes System Variable.
Sets the overall distance/dimensions for placing the op and bottom nailers.
Changes the location of the top nailer on the Z-axis (in/out). Tip: Used in conjunction with the variables NOY and NOX. Shapes System Variable.

Changes the dimension value of the height of the toe kick. Cut out on both the left and right side-panel of an item. Tip: The value of entered in the variables STHB and/or STHB2 will overwrite the visual aspect of the THB.
Changes the dimension value of the height of the front toe kick. Tip: Cut out on the left side-panel of an item.
Changes the dimension value of the height of the back toe kick. Tip: Cut out on the left side-panel of an item.
Changes the dimension of the top width of the item. Tip: Dimension change start from the front right of the item.

Changes the dimension of the second top depth. Tip: Dimension change starts from the front, it lengthens/shortens the front right top of an item. A positive value reduces the dimension, where as negative values increases the dimensions. Used in conjunction with the variable TPD.

Changes the location of the back of an item on the Z-axis (in/out). For corner/dialogical item changes the location of the first and second back. Tip: Positive values move the back(s) inwards while a negative value moves the back(s) outwards.
Clearance behind the cabinet back panel, when there are support brackets.
Changes the dimension of the second width of a corner/diagonal item, starting from the right.
Changes the height of the back corner/diagonal items. When applied it changes the second back of the item. Tip: Dimension change starts from the top of the item.
Changes the dimension value of the height of the front toe kick. Tip: Cut out on the right side-panel of an item.
Changes the dimension value of the height of the back toe kick. Tip: Cut out on the right side-panel of an item.
Changes the dimension value of the depth of a front toe kick. Tip: Cut out on the left side-panel of a n item.
Changes the dimension value of the depth of the back toe kick. Tip: Cut out on the left side-panel of an item.
Changes the dimension value of the depth of the front toe kick. Tip: Cut out on the right side-panel of an item.
Changes the dimension value of the depth of the back toe kick. Tip: Cut out on the right side-panel of an item.
Converts a front right clip corner to a front curved corner on an item. Tip: If value is set as 0, standard cut, if value is set as 1, curved corner. Used in conjunction with the variable CCR.
* Drawer rail origin Y 3.
Displays a clip corner on the back left of an item. Tip: If the value is set as 1, display clip corner, if value is set as 0, standard corner.
Display clip corner on the back right of an item. Tip: If the value is set as 1, display clip corner, if the value is set as 0, standard corner.

Displays or removes a recess pipe chase through the full height of an item. Tip: If the value entered is set as 1, pipe chase is displayed, if set as 0, standard item. Used in conjunction with the variables RPCW, RPCOW and RPCDP.
Displays /removes the door anchor of the traditional hinge number 1.
Display shelf location.
Sets the measure for the use of the system 32mm of the wood joining.

Changes the left side style of an item.If value is set as 0, then a standard side is displayed, if the value is set as 1, then a door panel matching the cabinet door is displayed without a side toe kick.

Changes the right side style of an item.If value is set as 0, then a standard side is displayed, if the value is set as 1 then a door panel matching the cabinet door is displayed without a side toe kick.
Changes the side depth adjustment. Tip: Values set as follows; 0 = back between top & bottom, 1 = top inside back, 2 = bottom inside back, 3 = top & bottom inside back.
Changes the style of the item top. Tip: Values set as follows; 0 = no top, 1 = corner blocks, 2 = back & front stretchers, 3 = front stretcher, 4 = full top, 5 = left & right stretchres.
Lowers/aligns the top to the value entered, in relation to the sides of the item. Tip: The greater the number the lower the top is moved down.
